text: Large language models, or LLMs, are advanced AI systems that understand and generate human-like text. They work by learning patterns from massive amounts of training data, including books, articles, and websites. This data helps the model predict the next word in a sentence, allowing it to write coherent paragraphs, answer questions, or translate languages. However, LLMs have limits—they can produce incorrect or biased information because they don't truly understand meaning. Users can improve results through prompt engineering, which involves carefully crafting inputs to guide the model. Despite their impressive capabilities, LLMs remain tools that reflect the strengths and weaknesses of their training data.
